name: Blk Bean Chili W/Spice Season
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the black beans overnight in water.
2. Drain the beans and place them in a large pot.
3. Add 8 cups of water and bring to a boil. Skim any foam that rises to the surface.
4. Add peppers, ginger, bay leaf, and cilantro. Reduce heat to low, cover, and gently simmer for 1 1/2 to 2 hours or until tender. Remove and discard the bay leaf.
5. Toast cumin seeds in a large saucepan over medium heat until darkened. Add chili powder, oregano, sun-dried tomatoes, and canned tomatoes with their juices. Stir well and bring mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes.
6. In another bowl, combine bulgur wheat with 1/2 cup boiling water, cover, and let stand for 10 minutes.
7. When beans are cooked, remove about 1 cup along with some liquid and puree in a blender.
8. Combine the puree with the remaining beans. Stir in the tomato mixture and bulgur. Season with salt and pepper and simmer for 10 minutes to heat.
9. To prepare spices: Place mustard seeds in a pot over medium heat, cover, and cook until seeds begin to pop. Drop fennel seeds into the pot, cover, and cook until popping stops and fennel darkens slightly (about 10-15 seconds).
10. Serve by pouring the spices over chili and adding the remaining cilantro to mix. Drizzle with olive oil.
11. Options: Use kidney or blackeye beans instead of black beans. Top with a scoop of yogurt.
